trade.summary: Bilateral trade collapsed after Russia's 2022 full-scale invasion of Ukraine. EU sanctions and German economic decoupling measures drastically reduced trade from pre-war levels of ~$80B/year. Remaining trade consists of limited non-sanctioned goods. Germany has essentially completed its energy decoupling from Russia, eliminating what was its largest trade component.

diplomatic.summary: Germany-Russia relations collapsed following Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine in February 2022. Germany expelled Russian diplomats, imposed sweeping sanctions, and reversed its longstanding policy of Ostpolitik. Germany is one of Ukraine's largest military and financial supporters. Russian hybrid attacks targeting Germany (cyberattacks, disinformation, sabotage) have further degraded relations. Diplomatic channels are minimal.
recent_incidents: Russian cyber attacks on German government (2023-2024)
Alleged Russian sabotage operations in Germany (2024)

military.summary: Germany and Russia are effectively in a proxy conflict in Ukraine. Germany provides substantial military aid to Ukraine to resist Russian aggression. Germany has terminated all military cooperation agreements with Russia. Russian nuclear threats have prompted Germany to increase defense spending dramatically (Zeitenwende). NATO-Russia relations are at their lowest point since the Cold War.
incidents: Russian military aggression against Ukraine, supported by Germany's aid (2022-present)

financial.summary: German firms have withdrawn from Russia. German banks froze Russian assets. Germany participated in G7 seizure of Russian sovereign assets. Nord Stream pipeline investment is a total loss. Russian oligarch assets in Germany are subject to sanctions and some seizure proceedings.

energy.summary: Germany has completed its decoupling from Russian energy. Nord Stream 1 and 2 are inoperable (sabotaged in September 2022). Germany built emergency LNG terminals in 2022-2023. Russian gas supply to Germany is zero. Russian oil embargo has been implemented. This decoupling represents the most dramatic energy policy reversal in German postwar history.

scientific.summary: Scientific cooperation has been suspended. German research institutions severed most ties with Russian counterparts. Russian scientists at German institutions face scrutiny. Joint publications have dropped sharply. German academic sanctions mirror broader Western scientific decoupling from Russia.
